questions: Should pending payment intents have a TTL/expiry? If the recipient never replies, should the intent auto-expire after N hours? If so, what timeout?
If the admin initiates a second payment for the same recipient in the same channel while one is already pending, should it overwrite the existing intent or reject as a duplicate?

For the LLM classification of confirmation replies (awaiting_confirmation state): is a lightweight Anthropic API call acceptable here, or should confirmation strictly use the existing PaymentConfirmView button only? The button approach is simpler and already identity-verified — the LLM classification would only be needed if we want to accept free-text confirmations instead of button clicks.

success_criteria.criterion: initiate_payment tool appears in TOOLS list and ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS set; the assertion at tools.py:916 passes
initiate_payment does NOT accept a wallet_address parameter
When recipient has a wallet on file: initiate_payment creates a test payment with producer='admin_chat', is_test=True and auto-confirms it
When no wallet on file: initiate_payment creates a pending intent, sends a channel message pinging the recipient, and returns status='awaiting_wallet'
initiate_payment fails closed when called without guild_id or source_channel_id (DM context)
initiate_payment fails closed on invalid recipient_user_id or amount_usd <= 0
Recipient wallet reply in originating channel is intercepted by _check_pending_payment_reply BEFORE _is_directed_at_bot and _can_user_message_bot gates
Invalid wallet reply rejects with error message and does NOT consume the intent
Valid wallet reply triggers upsert_wallet, request_payment(is_test=True), and auto-confirm
handle_payment_result for producer='admin_chat' test-confirmed creates final payment (is_test=False) and calls send_confirmation_request
handle_payment_result for test-failed does NOT create a final payment (fail-closed) and notifies admin
producer_ref includes timestamp or UUID to satisfy unique index on (producer, producer_ref, is_test)
Intent matching uses deterministic key (guild_id, channel_id, recipient_user_id) — identity-gated before any semantic classification
Ambiguous, suspicious, or out-of-ordinary reply patterns fail closed and tag the admin for review
Multiple concurrent intents in the same channel for different recipients are handled correctly (only the correct recipient's intent is matched)
source_channel_id is injected in agent.py using a key that does not conflict with existing tools
All existing tests pass: test_scheduler.py, test_social_route_tools.py, full suite
System prompt in agent.py documents initiate_payment including guild-channel requirement
New test file tests/test_admin_payments.py covers: both wallet paths, validation failures, wallet reply handling, terminal callbacks, and concurrent intents
execute_initiate_payment and _start_admin_payment_flow are each under ~80 lines with clear early-return error paths
handle_payment_result follows GrantsCog structure: guard → branch is_test → branch status
_check_pending_payment_reply has minimal overhead for non-matching messages (single dict lookup)
Race-condition guard (_processing_intents set) prevents double-processing of the same wallet reply
Manual smoke test: admin says 'pay @user $50' → bot pings user → user replies wallet → test queues → confirmation button appears → user clicks → payout executes

assumptions: Producer name is 'admin_chat' — PaymentCog._candidate_producer_cog_names('admin_chat') produces ['AdminChatCog', ...] matching the cog class name with zero PaymentCog changes.
Pending intents are in-memory (dict on AdminChatCog). Lost on bot restart; admin can re-initiate. No DB migration needed for v1.
Admin must initiate from a guild channel, not DM. Admin DMs have guild_id=None (AdminChatCog skips guild resolution for admins at line 212) and no channel to ping the recipient in.
The wallet-reply interceptor fires before _is_directed_at_bot() and _can_user_message_bot(). The recipient does NOT need can_message_bot permission or to @mention the bot.

Final-payment confirmation uses the existing PaymentConfirmView button (posted in-channel by send_confirmation_request). The button already enforces recipient_discord_id identity check. No free-text confirmation needed for v1 — the LLM classification in awaiting_confirmation state is only used if we move to text-based confirmation later.

For v1 the awaiting_confirmation LLM classification can be omitted — the PaymentConfirmView button handles confirmation. The intent transitions directly from test_in_progress to removed (button handles the rest). The LLM classification is documented as a future path for text-based confirmations per the revision lock.
No payment amount guardrail — admin is trusted.

handle_payment_result posts producer-specific messages with payment details to the notify channel. PaymentCog already posts its own generic terminal notification separately (same pattern as GrantsCog which also posts its own thread messages).
